title: GMS Week 05 - SLIPPERY AS A SNAKE!
section_path: GMS Week 05 - SLIPPERY AS A SNAKE!
section_path_text: GMS Week 05 - SLIPPERY AS A SNAKE!
summary:
Main
in Weekly Demolition Reports 03/02/2025
Image reference: 2025-02-03_gms-week-05-slippery-as-a-snake_img1.jpg
Despite a frantic start to 2025 that saw a marked return of recycling tonnage at the bidding tables, SNP activity across theglobal ship recycling spectrum eased further this week as Chinese New Year holidays descended upon the world. It is the "Year of the Snake" and it is indeed expected to be a far busier one than the preceding years, with record low volumes of supply and an ensuing inertia that eventually led to the shuttering of yards across the major global ship recycling destinations in 2024. Notwithstanding, should supply become unsustainable, this may apply even further pressure on recycling markets and eventually affect ship owners down the line, who will then have no choice but to bite the bullet of chasing falling prices in markets.
